findings.evidence: The first two movements prioritize familiar occupation atmosphere and baseline character work at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:3 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:97; the chapter's singular engine does not become active until the footnote discovery at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:185.
findings.problem: The opening is controlled and readable, but its novel-specific distinctiveness arrives too late. Until the Bagshot footnote, the chapter plays as competent Hogwarts-under-occupation setup rather than a chapter only this book could write, which keeps the award lens below shortlist level.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the footnote discovery incidental at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:185, but seed Gavin's sideways curiosity earlier in the train or feast material around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:27, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:63, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:141. Compress one existing atmosphere beat if needed so the earlier pages contain a concrete, character-specific snag with magic, books, notes, or classification that only Gavin notices.
findings.acceptance_test: Before the library discovery scene begins, the revised chapter contains at least one concrete moment in which Gavin's attention catches on a textual or theoretical oddity that other characters ignore; when the reader reaches snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:185 or its equivalent, the footnote feels like the escalation of an existing Gavin-specific attention pattern rather than the chapter's first novel-specific spark.

findings.evidence: The early-weeks block reports the Carrow regime in summary instead of scene at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:171, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:175, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:177.
findings.problem: This is sanitization by summary. Right when the chapter turns from arrival atmosphere to lived occupation, the prose steps back into report: Alecto's classroom, corridor fear, and Duncan's deterioration are described cleanly and accurately, but not put on the page as one embodied event with immediate social or bodily consequence. In this novel, that retreat from scene-presence is a high craft failure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Break open the summary block around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:171, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:175 by staging one complete classroom or corridor beat before the library scene at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:181. Give the reader an on-page exchange, one physical detail of the room, and one immediate consequence on Duncan, Gavin, or the surrounding students, then let the remaining weeks compress around that anchor.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passage replacing or expanding the material anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:171, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:175, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:177, the reader can point to one dramatized Carrow-era incident rather than only a retrospective overview: the passage includes quoted speech from the regime or a student response, at least one sensory detail tied to the room or corridor, and a visible immediate effect on a body or a social dynamic before the chapter moves to the library.

findings.evidence: The chapter repeatedly routes observation through the same comparison scaffold at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:65, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:107, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:169, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:175, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:183, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:217, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:219.
findings.problem: The repeated 'the way...' comparison template becomes a prose tic. Instead of each analogy feeling freshly pressured by the scene, the chapter starts to explain perception through the same sentence shape, especially in the back third, which flattens rhythm and makes Gavin's otherwise plain register feel mechanically patterned.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise several of the repeated comparison sentences at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:169, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:175, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:183, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:217, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:219 so some observations land as direct sensory fact or use different syntax. Keep the strongest one or two analogies; cut or restructure the rest rather than stacking similar 'the way...' turns.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised chapter, no more than two major observational sentences rely on the 'the way...' comparison scaffold, and the closing footnote sequence conveys Gavin's curiosity through a mix of direct statement, action, and at most one extended analogy rather than repeated analogical explanations.

summary: Gavin, Miren, and Duncan remain vocally distinct, contraction use matches the style bible, the chapter stays in past tense, and I found no continuity contradictions against the provided sheet. The chapter still fails overall because its singular engine arrives late, the first sustained look at occupied Hogwarts retreats into summary at the point where scene-presence is needed, and a repeated comparison template starts to flatten the prose.
